subject: Richmond guy here — built something for fence companies
bodyOverride:
I'm in the Richmond area myself, so I'll keep this neighborly. You've run a family fence business here for 10+ years — I built a tool I'd like to put in front of you first, before I take it wider.

FenceTrace gives homeowners a real fence estimate from a satellite photo of their yard — using YOUR material prices and YOUR labor rates — in about a minute. Leads come from your own website and referrals (not shared with anyone, no per-lead fee), and you see your profit on every job before the quote goes out.

It's month to month, no contract, and since we're local I'm happy to set your price book up for you personally — just reply and I'll take it from there.

Worth a look? Two-minute try: https://fencetrace.com

name: Jorge
subject: You personally handle every project — including every estimate?
bodyOverride:
Your site says you personally handle every project — which I'd bet means you're also personally driving to every estimate.

I run FenceTrace. It does the estimate from a satellite photo of the property: you (or the homeowner) trace the fence line, and it counts every post, rail, picket, and concrete bag — priced with YOUR material costs and YOUR labor rates. You see your profit on the job before you send the quote, and your customer can approve it from a link. The site visit becomes a confirmation, not a prerequisite.

No shared leads, no per-lead fees — these are your own website visitors and referrals. Month to month, no contract, and if you'd like a hand loading your prices, just reply — I'll set your price book up for you.

Worth a look? Two-minute try: https://fencetrace.com

name: Landon
subject: Your price-match guarantee needs a price book
bodyOverride:
You guarantee to match any competitor's price for the same quality — which only works if you know your numbers cold on every quote.

That's exactly what FenceTrace does: it keeps your price book — your material costs, labor rates by fence type, your markup, your job minimum — and prices every estimate from it automatically, off a satellite photo of the property, in about a minute. You see your profit and margin before the quote goes out, so matching a competitor never means accidentally working for free.

Your leads stay yours alone — no shared-lead fees, no bidding war. Month to month, no contract, and if you'd like a hand loading your prices, just reply.

Worth a look? Two-minute try: https://fencetrace.com

subject: You show homeowners the fence — but they still wait for a number
bodyOverride:
Saw that you use AR to show Greenville homeowners their fence before it's built — that's ahead of the curve. But it looks like they still have to wait for an on-site visit to get a price.

FenceTrace closes that gap: the homeowner (or you) traces the fence on a satellite photo of their property, and a real estimate prices out in about a minute — every post, rail, and picket, priced with YOUR material costs and YOUR labor rates. You see your profit before the quote goes out; they can approve it from a link. The site visit becomes a confirmation, not a bottleneck.

Month to month, no contract, and if you'd like a hand loading your prices, just reply — I'll set your price book up for you.

Worth a look? Two-minute try: https://fencetrace.com

company: Florida Fence of Tampa Inc (Tampa, FL)
subject: A few Tampa fence companies already do instant quotes
bodyOverride:
Serving Tampa Bay since '72 — not many fence companies can say that.

Here's why I'm writing: several Tampa-area fence companies have already added instant-quote tools to their websites. When a homeowner is comparing you against a site that shows a price in 60 seconds, "fill out our contact form" loses more often than it should.

I run FenceTrace. It gives homeowners a real fence estimate from a satellite photo of their yard — using YOUR material prices and YOUR labor rates — in about a minute. The leads come from your own website and referrals, they aren't shared with anyone, and there's no per-lead fee. You also see your profit on every job before you send the quote.

It's month to month, no contract, and if you'd like a hand loading your prices, just reply — I'll set your price book up for you.

Worth a look? Two-minute try: https://fencetrace.com

opener: Congrats on taking 2025 Bold City's Best for backyard construction — Jacksonville clearly knows your work. And heads up: a couple of Jacksonville fence companies have already added instant-quote tools to their websites.
